data.narrative:
Iambic Therapeutics is a Series B-stage biotech company that has raised $220M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 same-stage private peers in Biotech & Health (Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, Profluent) and 3 public sector comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two applicable triangulation methods inform the valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $220M total raised as a proxy baseline, yielding $418M. Peer-median analysis across the 5 private comparables produces an $810M implied valuation, with the peer range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 7.4x across the sector) is not applicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The two-method triangulation supports a range of $355M to $931M, with a median of $614M. The wide spread reflects material differences between the stage-progression method ($418M) and peer-median approach ($810M), likely driven by heterogeneity in the peer set—particularly the $3.3B outlier (Eikon) and the $600M floor (Profluent). Key uncertainties include the true comparability of peer valuations given varying technology maturity and clinical progress, and the absence of revenue data that would enable public-market anchoring. A valuation near the $614M median appears defensible absent additional operational or clinical milestones.
